visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid for Employment in Switzerland only. Only Kuwaiti nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa. It can only be submitted at the Swiss Embassy in Qatar. Visa applicants are requested to submit their complete application in person at the Swiss Embassy, Qatar.  Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted. Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application.

## Visa Fees
Please contact the Swiss Embassy in Qatar directly to know the Visa fees and to fix an appointment to submit your application form for a work visa.
The amount must be paid in cash in Qatari Riyals at the time of submitting the application.

## Documents Required
Please contact the Embassy of Switzerland in Qatar by email doh.visa@eda.admin.ch (mailto:doh.visa@eda.admin.ch), or visit website : Click here (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/qatar/en/home/representations/regional-consular-centre-gcc.html) in order to receive the information on the documentation to be submitted to apply for a “work visa”.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa. Click Here

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
Below attached is Application Form D (Long Term visa) to submit your application for a work visa.
Click here (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/visumantragsformular.html) to download Application Form D

visa_types.name: Visiting Family / Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Original application form duly completed, dated and signed. Note: For children under 18 years old, both parents must sign the application/declaration form. Passport copies of the father and mother have to be attached.
Original passport (signed original and 1 copy of signature and picture pages)
(Must be at least 3 months valid after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States).

Note: Please submit copies of previous visas.
Only legal residents of Kuwait with existing residency can apply.
2 recent passport photographs
Note: Passport size with white or light blue background; must be recent (not older than 6 months) and sized 45–50mm high and 35–40mm wide
Important note: It is important that the submitted photograph on your application form matches the image taken when your biometric information is being enrolled at the Visa Application Center.
To avoid inconvenience at the border control, please ensure that you submit a photograph which is not photoshopped/edited and present yourself during the biometric enrolment with less or subtle make-up which resembles the true likeness of you when you travel. 
Travel Insurance(copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30,000.
- Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight ticket: Please supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Ticket).
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Original up-to-date Bank statement (required for Kuwaiti only): including the current balance, or a salary statement or credit card copy and credit card statement for the last three months
- Original up-to-date Bank statement (required for non-Kuwaiti only): for the last six months.
Important reminder:
Must be printed on original letterhead of the issuing bank or stamped/attested by bank.For non-Kuwaiti nationals
Original Letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
Residency permit/ID card (copy): The end of validity shall be at least 3 months after the intended return date.
Trade license (copy)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once application is received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Switzerland, in Doha, the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre, Kuwait.

Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.

Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and the Visa fees must be paid in cash/knet at the Visa Application Center
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:

The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.

If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first stay.

Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Kuwait nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Kuwait. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.

Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and the Visa fees must be paid in cash/knet at the Visa Application Center
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:

The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.

If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first stay.

Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

## Visa Fees
Please Note: There is a service charge of 8.900 Kuwaiti Dinars per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants

[che > kwt > en > Table:Visiting Family / Friends > Visa Fees 1]
Please note :
Countries with a visa facilitation agreement with the European Union, the Schengen visa fee amounts to 13.000KWD, for national of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, FYROM, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine, and Republic of Yugoslavia.
All payments will be collected in local currency (cash/knet only).
Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation
If your payment transaction is successful but you did not receive confirmation, please do not attempt to create another transaction and contact us.
Kindly have Appointment confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.

The Visa fees must be paid in cash/knet at the Visa Application Center

Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice.

All fees are non-refundable.
Applying for Refund (Appointments cancelled 48 hrs prior to the scheduled date) :
Applicants are requested to please write to us on info.chkuw@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.chkuw@vfshelpline.com) for requesting refund with details and receipt of payment and reason for requesting refund. Kindly note it may take 15-30 working days for processing your refund once approved.
Please Note: VFS Service charges are non-refundable in case an appointment is a no show.

## Documents Required
Original application form duly completed, dated and signed. Note: For children under 18 years old, both parents must sign the application/declaration form. Passport copies of the father and mother have to be attached.
Original passport (signed original and 1 copy of signature and picture pages)
(Must be at least 3 months valid after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States).

Note: Please submit copies of previous visas.
Only legal residents of Kuwait with existing residency can apply.
2 recent passport photographs
Note: Passport size with white or light blue background; must be recent (not older than 6 months) and sized 45–50mm high and 35–40mm wide
Important note: It is important that the submitted photograph on your application form matches the image taken when your biometric information is being enrolled at the Visa Application Center.
To avoid inconvenience at the border control, please ensure that you submit a photograph which is not photoshopped/edited and present yourself during the biometric enrolment with less or subtle make-up which resembles the true likeness of you when you travel. 
Travel Insurance(copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30,000.
- Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight ticket: Please supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Ticket).
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Original up-to-date Bank statement (required for Kuwaiti only): including the current balance, or a salary statement or credit card copy and credit card statement for the last three months
- Original up-to-date Bank statement (required for non-Kuwaiti only): for the last six months.
Important reminder:
Must be printed on original letterhead of the issuing bank or stamped/attested by bank.For non-Kuwaiti nationals
Original Letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
Residency permit/ID card (copy): The end of validity shall be at least 3 months after the intended return date.
Trade license (copy)

## Photo Specifications
- Photos must be no more than 6 months old 
- 45–50mm high and 35–40mm wide
- Must show head and top of shoulders close up, so that the face occupies
70% to 80% of the photo area
- Must be perfectly on focus
- Must be printed on high-quality paper with high resolution and
should not present ink stains or folds
- Photograph cannot be manipulated, for example, by removing spots
or softening lines.

- Background should be white or light blue 
- Luminosity shall be uniform, without shades, flash reflections or red eye effect
- Colors shall be natural
- Eyes shall be open and perfectly visible – with no hair across them
- Face shall be straight and looking at the camera. You shall not look over
one shoulder and your head shall not be tilted (portrait style)
- Must show clearly both sides of your face

The photograph must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show natural skin tones
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast, and natural colors
- Be printed on high resolution photographic paper

If you wear glasses, they must show your eyes clearly:
- No flash reflection off the lenses.
- No dark tinted lenses
- Lighter framed glasses are preferable
- Frame should not cover any part of your eyes
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons:
- in which case, facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and
both edges of your face must be clearly shown
- One person per photograph (without furniture, toys, objects, or
another person, visible), showing you looking directly at the camera with
a neutral expression
                                                                                            and mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents  to the Visa Application Center.  You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application form. 
Click here (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/visumantragsformular.html) to download Application form
Checklist
Parental Authorisation (for children under the age of 18 years)
Please note that for the children below 18 years, all the above documents needs to be signed by both the Parents.

## Photo Specifications
- Photos must be no more than 6 months old 
- 45–50mm high and 35–40mm wide
- Must show head and top of shoulders close up, so that the face occupies
70% to 80% of the photo area
- Must be perfectly on focus
- Must be printed on high-quality paper with high resolution and
should not present ink stains or folds
- Photograph cannot be manipulated, for example, by removing spots
or softening lines.

- Background should be white or light blue 
- Luminosity shall be uniform, without shades, flash reflections or red eye effect
- Colors shall be natural
- Eyes shall be open and perfectly visible – with no hair across them
- Face shall be straight and looking at the camera. You shall not look over
one shoulder and your head shall not be tilted (portrait style)
- Must show clearly both sides of your face

The photograph must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show natural skin tones
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast, and natural colors
- Be printed on high resolution photographic paper

If you wear glasses, they must show your eyes clearly:
- No flash reflection off the lenses.
- No dark tinted lenses
- Lighter framed glasses are preferable
- Frame should not cover any part of your eyes
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons:
- in which case, facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and
both edges of your face must be clearly shown
- One person per photograph (without furniture, toys, objects, or
another person, visible), showing you looking directly at the camera with
a neutral expression
                                                                                            and mouth closed.

Application form
- 3 forms per applicant duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required.
- For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.

- 3 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.

- Proof of financial means for the duration of the requested stay in Switzerland (e.g. bank statement of applicant or her/his parents etc.)
- Diploma of already completed studies
- Written commitment to leave Switzerland after the requested stay
- Letter of motivation which must include
- the reason for studying in Switzerland
- the future plans of the applicant after the termination of the studies in Switzerland
- For all of the above mentioned documents, two photocopies have to be supplied as well as the original document.
- The Embassy will only accept complete files for further proceeding.

Important note: It is important that the submitted photograph on your application form matches the image taken when your biometric information is being enrolled at the Visa Application Center.

- Photos must be no more than 6 months old

- Must show head and top of shoulders close up, so that the face occupies

- Must be perfectly on focus
- Must be printed on high-quality paper with high resolution and
should not present ink stains or folds
- Photograph cannot be manipulated, for example, by removing spots
or softening lines.

- Background should be white or light blue 
- Luminosity shall be uniform, without shades, flash reflections or red eye effect
- Colors shall be natural
- Eyes shall be open and perfectly visible – with no hair across them
- Face shall be straight and looking at the camera. You shall not look over
one shoulder and your head shall not be tilted (portrait style)
- Must show clearly both sides of your face

The photograph must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show natural skin tones
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast, and natural colors
- Be printed on high resolution photographic paper

If you wear glasses, they must show your eyes clearly:
- No flash reflection off the lenses.
- No dark tinted lenses
- Lighter framed glasses are preferable
- Frame should not cover any part of your eyes
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons:
- in which case, facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and
both edges of your face must be clearly shown
- One person per photograph (without furniture, toys, objects, or
another person, visible), showing you looking directly at the camera with
a neutral expression
                                                                                            and mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
